subject: Sociology
title: Courts and Sentencing
description: A short lesson to help you apply Courts and Sentencing.

hook.text: Two people commit the same crime. One gets probation, the other gets five years. Same act, different fates.

buildup.text: Courts translate laws into punishment. Judges, juries, and prosecutors hold enormous power in that translation.

discovery.text: Sentencing disparities correlate with race, class, and gender. Justice is not as blind as the statue suggests.

twist.text: Plea bargains resolve 95% of US cases. Most defendants never see a trial — they negotiate under pressure.

climax.text: The court system processes people, not cases. Who you are shapes your sentence as much as what you did.

punchline.text: Justice weighs the person, not just the crime.

quiz.question: What do sentencing disparities reveal?
options: Race, class, and gender influence sentencing outcomes
